methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: packHeader: encodes timeBounds + afterDelayedMessagesRead into 40-byte header
formCallDataHash: keccak256(header ++ data)
formBlobDataHash: keccak256(header ++ DATA_BLOB_HEADER_FLAG ++ packed(dataHashes))
blobCost normalization: blobBaseFee * GAS_PER_BLOB * count / block.basefee
data_flows.trust_changes: User input (data) becomes authenticated on-chain via batch poster role check and data hash commitment
exposure_risks: Calldata is publicly visible on L1
Blob data hashes are publicly readable via reader4844
manipulation_risks: Batch poster can choose any valid data flag header byte
sequenceNumber ~uint256(0) bypass allows any sequence number
DAS keyset hash validation only if DAS header flag set and data.length >= 33

path: forceInclusion called with delayed message parameters
If isDelayBufferable: buffer.update(l1BlockAndTime[0]) then compute delayBufferableBlocks
transformations: Messages.messageHash reconstructs message hash from kind, sender, block, time, nonce, baseFeeL1, messageDataHash
Messages.accumulateInboxMessage computes accumulator from prevAcc + messageHash
buffer.update modifies buffer state based on delayed message block number
data_flows.trust_changes: Anyone can trigger but message is validated against bridge delayed inbox accumulator - no trust escalation possible
validation_points: Block number delay check (l1BlockAndTime[0] + delayBlocks_ < block.number)
manipulation_risks: MISSING timestamp-based delay check (ForceIncludeTimeTooSoon error defined but never used) - only block-based check enforced
Buffer update with user-supplied l1BlockAndTime[0] could affect delay buffer state for subsequent operations

path: delayProofImpl: validate delay proof against delayed accumulator
transformations: Messages.isValidDelayedAccPreimage validates proof against accumulator
buffer.update: calcPendingBuffer then store new prevBlockNumber and prevSequencedBlockNumber
data_flows.trust_changes: Delay proof validated against on-chain accumulator before buffer modification
manipulation_risks: If buffer.isUpdatable() returns false (synced AND full), the proof is silently skipped and buffer is not updated
delayProof.delayedMessage.blockNumber directly used in buffer.update - validated only via accumulator check

transformations: safeTransferFrom depositor to pool on deposit
safeIncreaseAllowance from pool to rollup/challengeManager
newStakeOnNewAssertion / createLayerZeroEdge transfers tokens from pool
withdrawStakerFunds returns tokens to pool
safeTransfer from pool to depositor on withdrawal
data_flows.trust_changes: Depositors trust the pool contract to correctly manage stake lifecycle; pool trusts rollup/challengeManager to return stake on confirmation
exposure_risks: Tokens sent directly to pool contract are lost (documented)
Any excess tokens beyond deposited amount stuck in pool forever (documented)
manipulation_risks: AssertionStakingPool.createAssertion uses user-supplied requiredStake from assertionInputs for allowance - if config hash validation in rollup fails, allowance remains increased
Total deposited can exceed required stake - early withdrawals possible even after protocol move
No reentrancy guard in withdrawFromPool, though CEI pattern is followed

path: cleanupOldRollup: pause, refund stakers (max 50), upgrade secondary logic
createConfig: fetch latest confirmed state from PREIMAGE_LOOKUP
upgradeSurroundingContracts: upgrade bridge, sequencer inbox, inbox, REI, outbox
Initialize challenge manager with expected rollup address
Initialize rollup, set validators, transfer ownership
transformations: StateHashPreImageLookup converts state hash to ExecutionState + inboxMaxCount
ExecutionState converted to AssertionState for genesis
Config constructed with immutable parameters from BOLDUpgradeAction
sinks: Upgraded bridge contracts pointing to new rollup
Challenge manager initialized with new rollup
data_flows.trust_changes: Upgrade executor must be trusted to provide correct PREIMAGE_LOOKUP data and validator list before upgrade execution
validation_points: ROLLUP_READER.isValidator check for each new validator
exposure_risks: StateHashPreImageLookup.set is permissionless - anyone can set pre-images (but validated against hash)
Window between upgradeSurroundingContracts and rollup deployment where bridge points to non-existent contract (within same tx)
manipulation_risks: Only 50 stakers can be refunded in cleanupOldRollup - remaining stakers must manually withdraw
If PREIMAGE_LOOKUP is not populated before upgrade, PREIMAGE_LOOKUP.get reverts (inboxMaxCount == 0 check)
createConfig uses empty maxTimeVariation and bufferConfig - relies on SequencerInbox being upgraded in place

path: createLayerZeroEdge: validator creates edge with stake
refundStake: recover stake for confirmed edge
transformations: Edge store creates/tracks edges with unique IDs based on level, origin, heights, history roots
Timer cache accumulates unrivaled time through parent-child chain
Stake routing: first edge stake kept in contract, rival stakes sent to excessStakeReceiver
data_flows.trust_changes: Validators are authenticated via whitelist (if enabled); edge creation requires stake as sybil resistance
validation_points: Assertion hash validation for block-level edges
History root and prefix proof validation during bisection
One step proof verification for final confirmation
manipulation_risks: First edge in a rival group keeps stake in contract; if it loses, stake is NOT sent to excessStakeReceiver - stays locked unless protocol has mechanism to recover
Timer manipulation via strategic edge creation timing
assertionBlocks time credit only given to first child of predecessor

core_rules: Only batch posters can submit L2 batches to the SequencerInbox
Force inclusion is permissionless but requires the sequencer-only delay window to expire
Delay buffer depletes when delayed messages experience unexpected delay (beyond threshold)
Buffer saturation: minimum at threshold, maximum at max
Assertions must consume at least one inbox message (non-empty assertions)
Only one assertion in a rival group can be confirmed
Stakers must be inactive (latestConfirmed or has children) before stake withdrawal
Edge confirmation requires cumulative unrivaled time >= challengePeriodBlocks
economic_incentives: Batch posters receive spending reports for gas reimbursement on L2 (only for ETH-based chains)
Edge creators must stake tokens; losing stakes go to excessStakeReceiver for honest party reimbursement
Staking pools allow permissionless participation without running validator infrastructure
Force inclusion mechanism ensures liveness - sequencer cannot censor indefinitely
Delay buffer mechanism provides adaptive censorship resistance
governance_mechanisms: Rollup owner (via UpgradeExecutor) can update maxTimeVariation, buffer config, validators, batch posters
Batch poster manager can rotate batch poster keys without rollup owner
Security council / rollup owner can pause rollup and force-confirm assertions when paused
DAS keyset management: owner can add/invalidate keysets

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Force inclusion should require both block AND time delays to expire (dual check)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only block-based delay (l1BlockAndTime[0] + delayBlocks_ >= block.number) is checked. ForceIncludeTimeTooSoon error is defined but never used.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: On chains where block production rate varies or is faster than expected, the sequencer-only window could expire earlier by time than intended. An attacker could force-include messages before the intended time-based delay, potentially during periods of fast block production.

edge_cases.scenario: Buffer update skipped when isUpdatable returns false
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Buffer should always be updated when new delayed messages are processed
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In delayProofImpl, if buffer.isUpdatable() returns false (synced AND full), the entire buffer update including delay proof validation is skipped
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: When the buffer is synced and full, a batch poster can post batches with new delayed messages without providing valid delay proofs. This is likely by design since no depletion can occur in this state, but it means delay proof validation is bypassed.

edge_cases.scenario: BOLDUpgradeAction cleanupOldRollup caps staker refunds at 50
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All existing stakers should be refunded during upgrade
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only first 50 stakers are processed in the for-loop; remaining stakers must manually withdraw after upgrade via patched user logic
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Remaining stakers' funds are not lost (they can withdraw via patched logic) but they experience degraded UX and may not be aware they need to manually withdraw

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Allowance should match exactly the required stake from the rollup's configuration
edge_cases.actual_behavior: requiredStake is taken from user-supplied assertionInputs.beforeStateData.configData.requiredStake and used in safeIncreaseAllowance before config hash is validated by the rollup
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the rollup call reverts, the transaction reverts and allowance is unchanged. If a very large requiredStake passes config validation, only the correct amount is transferred. The risk is mitigated by the rollup's configHash validation.

edge_cases.scenario: Blob gas cost calculation with zero basefee
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Batch poster should be fairly reimbursed for blob posting costs
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When block.basefee is 0, blobCost / block.basefee returns 0, meaning no gas credit for blob costs in spending report
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: On chains where basefee can be zero (e.g., some L2s or custom chains), batch posters receive no blob cost reimbursement. However, blobs are explicitly not supported on Arbitrum host chains.

edge_cases.scenario: EdgeChallengeManager first edge stake stuck on loss
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All losing stakes should be recoverable or redistributed
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The first edge created in a rival group has its stake held in the contract. If this edge loses, the stake cannot be refunded (refundStake likely requires confirmed status). Subsequent rivals' stakes are sent to excessStakeReceiver immediately.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Over many challenges, stuck stakes could accumulate in the EdgeChallengeManager contract. The excessStakeReceiver mechanism is designed to handle redistribution of excess stakes, but first-edge losing stakes are not routed there.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Buffer should replenish at the configured rate
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Replenishment uses integer division: (elapsed * replenishRateInBasis) / BASIS. For small elapsed values and small replenishRateInBasis, the replenishment rounds down to zero.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A sequencer posting frequent small batches could prevent buffer replenishment if each batch interval is too small. This is bounded by the requirement of actual delayed messages and gas costs.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Sequence numbers should be strictly validated
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If sequenceNumber == ~uint256(0) (type(uint256).max), the sequence number check is skipped entirely
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: This is an intentional design to allow flexible batch posting, but it means batch posters can submit with any effective sequence number. The bridge's accumulator ensures ordering correctness regardless.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Owner should always be the intended party
edge_cases.actual_behavior: During createRollup, the RollupCreator sets itself as owner temporarily, then transfers to upgradeExecutor. During BOLDUpgradeAction.perform, BOLDUpgradeAction sets itself as owner temporarily.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Since all operations happen within a single transaction, there's no window for exploitation. If the transaction reverts mid-way, all state changes are rolled back.

edge_cases.scenario: forceInclusion with delay buffer proactively updates buffer before delay check
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Buffer update should not make force inclusion harder for legitimate force-includers
edge_cases.actual_behavior: buffer.update(l1BlockAndTime[0]) is called before the delay check. If the update depletes the buffer, the effective delay (delayBufferableBlocks) becomes smaller, potentially making force inclusion easier. Conversely, if it replenishes, force inclusion becomes harder.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A strategic force-includer could choose which delayed message to force include in order to manipulate the buffer update in their favor, potentially enabling earlier force inclusion of subsequent messages.

edge_cases.scenario: setBufferConfig can change buffer parameters while system is active
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Buffer config changes should not disrupt ongoing delay tracking
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _setBufferConfig adjusts bufferBlocks based on new max/threshold and calls buffer.update(block.number) if synced. This could reset prevBlockNumber and prevSequencedBlockNumber to current block, effectively resetting the delay tracking.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Rollup owner (via upgrade executor) could reset delay tracking by changing buffer config. This is an admin-only action and requires trust in governance.

assumption_violations: Assumption: block.number correlates with time for force inclusion delay - violated if block production rate changes significantly
Assumption: stakeToken is a well-behaved ERC20 without fee-on-transfer or rebasing - if violated, staking pool accounting breaks
Assumption: bridge.delayedMessageCount() and sequencerMessageCount() cannot decrease - if bridge is compromised, accounting breaks
Assumption: block.chainid is immutable in practice - chain ID changes trigger safety mode (1-block delays) via _chainIdChanged()
Assumption: reader4844 returns correct blob data hashes - if compromised, invalid batch data could be committed
Assumption: rollup owner / upgrade executor acts honestly - many critical parameters can be changed unilaterally
game_theory_risks: Strategic force inclusion timing: Anyone can force-include to manipulate delay buffer state
Batch poster can strategically time delay proofs to optimize buffer state
Edge challenge: Creating rivals has cost (stake), but first-edge creator's stake behavior differs from subsequent rivals, creating asymmetric incentives
Staking pool depositors can free-ride on others' deposits and withdraw early if pool exceeds required stake
Sequencer can delay including delayed messages up to the delay window without penalty (design intent)
integration_risks: reader4844 external call for blob data hashes - must be trusted or verified
ArbGasInfo precompile for L1 gas fees on Arbitrum host chains
ArbSys precompile for block numbers on Arbitrum host chains
Bridge contract trust: SequencerInbox trusts bridge for delayed inbox accumulators and message counts
IGasRefunder: refundsGas modifier calls external gas refunder which could be malicious (bounded by gas refund amount)
IERC20Bridge.nativeToken() try-catch during initialization could mask unexpected errors
calculation_errors: No mathematical overflow possible in DelayBuffer.calcBuffer since all inputs are uint64 cast to uint256, and replenishRateInBasis <= 10000
formBlobDataHash: blobCost = reader4844.getBlobBaseFee() * GAS_PER_BLOB * dataHashes.length could theoretically overflow for extreme blob base fees, though practically bounded
submitBatchSpendingReport: l1Fees / block.basefee could truncate significant digits when basefee is high relative to l1Fees

token_semantics.notes: Used for validator staking and edge creation. SafeERC20 is used throughout. Protocol assumes standard ERC20 behavior - fee-on-transfer tokens would break staking pool accounting. The token is configurable per rollup deployment.
Custom fee token for ERC20-based rollups. Used via IERC20Bridge interface. SafeERC20 used in RollupCreator for factory deployment fees.

invariants.name: delayed_messages_monotonic
invariants.description: totalDelayedMessagesRead can only increase and must never exceed bridge.delayedMessageCount()

invariants.name: buffer_bounds
invariants.description: buffer.threshold <= buffer.bufferBlocks <= buffer.max must hold after any buffer update

invariants.name: sequencer_accumulator_ordering
invariants.description: Sequencer message count strictly increases with each batch; accumulator chain is append-only

invariants.name: staking_pool_balance_conservation
invariants.description: Sum of all deposit balances should not exceed the pool's token balance. Can be less if stake is currently locked in rollup/challenge manager.

invariants.name: assertion_status_progression
invariants.description: Assertion status can only transition: NoAssertion -> Pending -> Confirmed. No reverse transitions allowed.

invariants.name: single_confirmation_per_rival_group
invariants.description: At most one edge per mutual ID can be confirmed. Once set, confirmedRival cannot change.

invariants.name: delay_buffer_effective_delay
invariants.description: Effective force inclusion delay = min(buffer.bufferBlocks, delayBlocks) when delay bufferable
